Questions:
I am analyzing the quarterly real GDP growth of Australia. The task is to compare the average quarterly real GDP growth rate, expressed as a percentage change from the previous quarter, between two defined periods.
The pre-pandemic period covers the time from the second quarter of 2017 (Q2 2017) through the fourth quarter of 2019 (Q4 2019), inclusive. The post-pandemic period begins with the first full quarter that follows the World Health Organization’s official announcement declaring the end of the global health emergency related to COVID-19 and continues through the second quarter of 2025 (Q2 2025), inclusive.
For each period, calculate the simple arithmetic mean of the quarterly real GDP growth rates, then compute the difference as the post-pandemic average minus the pre-pandemic average.
Return the result in the following JSON format, where the value is expressed in percentage points rounded to two decimal places: {"Difference": value}
Steps: 1. Search for 'Australia GDP quarterly growth' to identify the official source of quarterly GDP data

2. Navigate to the Australian Bureau of Statistics website at https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/economy/national-accounts/australian-national-accounts-national-income-expenditure-and-product/latest-release
3. Download the dataset containing 'Gross domestic product, chain volume measures, seasonally adjusted' values for all available quarters
4. Search for the official World Health Organization announcement that declared the end of the global health emergency related to COVID-19 and determine the first full quarter following that date

5. Extract the quarterly real GDP growth rates from Q2 2017 through Q4 2019 for the pre-pandemic period, and from the first full quarter after the WHO announcement through Q2 2025 for the post-pandemic period
6. Calculate the simple arithmetic mean of the quarterly GDP growth rates for each of the two periods
7. Compute the difference between the post-pandemic average and the pre-pandemic average, expressing the result in percentage points rounded to two decimal places

4. For each year from 1981 to 2019, calculate the difference between that year’s total and the previous year’s total
5. Identify the year with the largest increase and the year with the largest decrease in abortion numbers

Questions: I am analyzing the gender gap in labour force participation rates in Australia between August 1966 and September 2025. The task is to calculate the change in the gender gap, defined as the difference between the male and female participation rates in August 1966 and the difference between the male and female participation rates in September 2025. Compute the change as the difference between these two gender gaps, expressed in percentage points. Present the result in JSON format as follows: {"Change in Gender Gap": value} where the value represents the change in percentage points, rounded to two decimal places.
Steps: 1. Search for 'Australia labour force participation rates by gender' to locate official data sources
2. Open the detailed release available at https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/labour/employment-and-unemployment/labour-force-australia/latest-release
3. Retrieve the table that contains male and female labour force participation rates for August 1966 and September 2025

Questions: I am analyzing quarterly population dynamics in Australia from 2004 to 2024. For each quarter within this period, determine whether total population growth for that quarter was lower than the natural increase for the same quarter. Identify all quarters in which this condition is met and group them by year. Present the results in JSON format, where each key is a year and each value is a list of the quarters (e.g., "Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4") in that year for which total population growth was lower than the natural increase. Only include years that contain at least one such quarter.
Steps: 1. Search for 'ABS quarterly population components Australia' to locate official data
3. Download the tables containing quarterly total population growth and natural increase for Australia from 2004 to 2024
4. Extract the quarterly values for total population growth and natural increase
5. Identify all quarters where total population growth is lower than natural increase and record their year and quarter

Questions: I am analyzing monthly migration data for New Zealand from 2001 to 2020 to identify unusual patterns in migration. Only include years that contain at least one month with negative net migration.
Steps: 1. Search for 'Stats NZ monthly net migration' to locate the official data source
3. Find the table 'Estimated net migration, monthly, January 2001–September 2025'
4. Count, for each year between 2001 and 2020, the months with net migration below 0

Questions: I am analyzing New Zealand’s shifting immigration policies around 2021, when the government reduced low-skill immigration and redirected the system toward wealthier or higher-skilled migrants. For the countries China, India, South Africa, Fiji, Sri Lanka, and the Philippines, find the monthly net migration (estimate) values for each country in July 2019 and in July 2024. Present the results in JSON format, where each key is a country name and each value is an object containing two fields: "July 2019" and "July 2024".
Steps: 1. Search for 'Stats NZ monthly net migration by country' to find the official data source
3. Download the file ‘International migration: September 2025 – citizenship by visa, by country of last permanent residence – CSV’
4. Extract from the dataset the monthly net migration values for China, India, South Africa, Fiji, Sri Lanka, and the Philippines for July 2019 and July 2024

Questions: I am analyzing gender-based unemployment trends in New Zealand. Using official quarterly unemployment rate data for males and females from 2012 to 2024, identify all quarters in which the female unemployment rate was lower than the male unemployment rate. Present the results in JSON format, where each key is a year and each value is a list of the quarters (e.g., "Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4") that meet this condition. Only include years that contain at least one qualifying quarter.
Steps: 1. Search for 'Stats NZ unemployment rate by sex quarterly' to locate the dataset
2. Open the official Stats NZ unemployment rate page: https://www.stats.govt.nz/indicators/unemployment-rate/
3. On the page, locate the section that provides quarterly male and female unemployment rates
4. Identify all quarters in which the female unemployment rate was lower than the male unemployment rate
